subsection.heading: Critical failure at a reserved or transferred work

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: A reserved or transferred work shall only be eligible for funding under section 3201(2)(A) of this title if—

subparagraph.content: construction of the reserved or transferred work began on or before January 1, 1915; and

a unit of the reserved or transferred work suffered a critical failure in Bureau of Reclamation infrastructure during the 2-year period ending on November 15, 2021, that resulted in the failure to deliver water to project beneficiaries.

content.p: Rehabilitation, repair, and replacement activities for a transferred or reserved work using amounts made available under section 3201(2)(A) of this title may be used for the entire transferred or reserved work, regardless of whether the critical failure was limited to a single project of the overall work.
Notwithstanding section 510b(b) of this title, amounts made available to a reserved or transferred work under section 3201(2)(A) of this title shall be nonreimbursable to the United States.

subsection.heading: Carey Act projects
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary shall use amounts made available under section 3201(2)(B) of this title to fund the rehabilitation, reconstruction, or replacement of a dam—

paragraph.content: the construction of which began on or after January 1, 1905;
that was developed pursuant to section 4 of the Act of August 18, 1894 (commonly known as the “Carey Act”) (43 U.S.C. 641; 28 Stat. 422, chapter 301);

paragraph.chapeau: that the Governor of the State in which the dam is located has—

subparagraph.content: determined the dam has reached its useful life;
determined the dam poses significant health and safety concerns; and

paragraph.content: for which the estimated rehabilitation, reconstruction, or replacement, engineering, and permitting costs would exceed $50,000,000.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The Carey Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is Aug. 18, 1894, ch. 301, § 4, 28 Stat. 422, which is classified to section 641 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.
